page: Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is a compute-oriented virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Text processing performance is particularly notable, ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput fall within the average range. However, memory bandwidth for large, uncached blocks is a relative bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% at 38.06 GB/sec, which may impact memory-intensive workloads.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D8als_v6 offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated AMD EPYC cores make it suitable for tasks such as text processing, single-threaded decompression, lightweight web serving, and moderate computational tasks. It is less suited for large-scale in-memory databases or heavy LLM prompt processing due to its memory bandwidth limitations and 16 GB RAM capacity.
description: The Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Dalv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. This instance delivers strong single-core and text processing performance, with benchmarks placing its single-core capabilities in the top 25% and text processing in the top 10%. However, its large-block memory read bandwidth is relatively weak, falling into the bottom 25%. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is a cost-efficient option for compute-focused workloads that require dedicated CPU resources but have modest memory requirements. It is well-suited for text processing, lightweight web serving, and single-threaded data decompression tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 16 GB of RAM, strong single-core performance, and is optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D8als_v6 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine with 16 GB RAM and a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong single-core performance for memory-lean, CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz
Configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Delivers strong single-core and top-tier text processing performance
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage integration
Best suited for text processing, web serving, and single-threaded tasks
